level_signals: Minimum 4 years in building and launching high-quality consumer products
Base pay range $166K-$195K in Zone 1, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum 4 years in building and launching high-quality consumer products.
Proven experience in defining and developing AI-powered products.
Familiarity with experimentation platforms and A/B testing for ML systems.
Understanding of fine-tuning, evaluations, and ML optimization techniques.
Exposure to LLMs, recommender systems, NLP, or reinforcement learning.
Strong leadership, communication, and relationship management abilities.
Adept at translating complex technical details into easily understandable language for a wider audience.
Experience utilizing research, experimentation, and product analytics to drive informed decisions.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bonus points for experience in fintech and/or consumer AI products.
Experience with products leveraging Agentic AI is a plus.

scope.surface: AI Platform — secure AI foundation including model governance, secure model deployment, adversarial testing, AI risk controls, and monitoring frameworks
core_responsibilities: Set the strategy and roadmap for secure AI platform capabilities, including model governance, access controls, secure data pipelines, and production monitoring.
Define requirements for AI security standards such as model risk assessments, red-teaming, adversarial testing, explainability reviews, and audit logging.
Partner with Security, Privacy, Risk, and Compliance to translate regulatory expectations into clear product requirements and reusable platform controls.
Establish measurable health metrics for AI systems, including model performance, drift detection, abuse signals, and incident response readiness.
Guide internal teams through secure AI adoption by providing structured intake processes, risk reviews, and launch readiness criteria.

compensation.equity_form: equity ownership mentioned as part of performance-driven compensation

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed across three geographic zones
Clear product surface and ownership scope defined (AI Platform, model governance, security controls)
Named cross-functional partners (Security, Privacy, Risk, Compliance, Engineering, Data Science)
Equity ownership and bonus programs mentioned
Comprehensive benefits described including 100% paid health insurance for employees
red_flags: Hybrid requirement is 3 days per week in Bellevue, WA — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; unclear for international applicants
Role spans both security platform infrastructure and consumer-facing AI adoption — very broad scope for one PM
Technical requirements (LLMs, fine-tuning, adversarial testing, ML optimization) are listed as 'Technical Knowledge' bullets but severity classification is ambiguous — could be hard or soft

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated as KPIs in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
compensation.base_range_usd uses Zone 1 (Bellevue, WA) as the role's stated primary location
ambiguities: Technical Knowledge bullets (experimentation platforms, fine-tuning, LLMs, etc.) are listed under 'What you bring' without explicit Required/Preferred labels. Treated as hard must-haves given the role's core focus on secure AI, but they could reasonably be soft given the JD's overall framing.

Agentic AI and fintech experience are explicitly called out as bonus/plus, so classified as nice-to-haves; all other Technical Knowledge items are classified as hard given absence of softening language.

Compensation zones listed for multiple cities beyond Bellevue (Menlo Park, New York, Washington DC in Zone 1; Denver, Westlake, Chicago in Zone 2; Florida cities in Zone 3), but JD body states role is based in Bellevue, WA — it is unclear whether other locations are eligible for this specific role.
